    static LocalDate
    getMaxMinLocalDate(LocalDate d1, LocalDate d2, boolean max)
    Returns the maximum or minimum of two LocalDate values.

    Returns the maximum or minimum of two LocalDateTime values.

    isTodayOrAfter(LocalDate date)
    Checks if a given LocalDate is today or any day after today.
    static boolean
    isUntilDateEqualNowOrAfterOrInActualWeekend(LocalDate date)
    Checks if a given date is today, or after today, or was part of the most recent weekend (Saturday or Sunday) but only if today is Monday.
    static long
    LocalDateToEpocheSeconds(LocalDate localDate)
    Converts a LocalDate object to the number of seconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 GMT).

  • Constructor Details

    • DateHelper

      public DateHelper()

  • Method Details

    • getMaxMinLocalDate

      public static LocalDate getMaxMinLocalDate(LocalDate d1, LocalDate d2, boolean max)
      Returns the maximum or minimum of two LocalDate values. If one date is null, the other is returned. If both are null, null is returned.
      Parameters:
      d1 - The first date.
      d2 - The second date.
      max - If true, returns the maximum of the two dates; otherwise, returns the minimum.
      Returns:
      The maximum or minimum date, or one of the dates if the other is null, or null if both are null.

    • getMaxMinLocalDateTime

      public static LocalDateTime getMaxMinLocalDateTime(LocalDateTime d1, LocalDateTime d2, boolean max)
      Returns the maximum or minimum of two LocalDateTime values. If one is null, the other is returned. If both are null, null is returned.

    • isTodayOrAfter

      public static boolean isTodayOrAfter(LocalDate date)
      Checks if a given LocalDate is today or any day after today.
      Parameters:
      date - The date to check.
      Returns:
      true if the date is today or in the future, false otherwise.

    • isUntilDateEqualNowOrAfterOrInActualWeekend

      public static boolean isUntilDateEqualNowOrAfterOrInActualWeekend(LocalDate date)
      Checks if a given date is today, or after today, or was part of the most recent weekend (Saturday or Sunday) but only if today is Monday.
      Parameters:
      date - The date to check.
      Returns:
      true if the condition is met, false otherwise.

    • LocalDateToEpocheSeconds

      public static long LocalDateToEpocheSeconds(LocalDate localDate)
      Converts a LocalDate object to the number of seconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 GMT). The conversion uses the start of the day in the system's default time zone.
      Parameters:
      localDate - The LocalDate object to convert.
      Returns:
      The number of seconds since the epoch.
